(1)
Desired consumption function is as follows -
C = 400 + 0.8YD
YD in the consumption function indicates disposable national income.
The coefficient of disposable national income is the marginal propensity to spend.
So,
The marginal propensity to spend is 0.8.
(2)
Calculate the simple multiplier -
Simple multiplier = 1/[1 - marginal propensity to spend]
Simple multiplier = 1/[1 - 0.8] = 1/0.2 = 5
The Simple Multiplier is 5.
(3)
Equilibrium is attained when national income equals aggregate demand.
AD = C + I + G + X - IM
So,
At equilibrium
Y = C + I + G + X - IM
Y = 400 + 0.8YD + 100 + 500 + 650 - 0.3Y
Y = 400 + 0.8(Y - T) + 1,250 - 0.3Y
Y = 1,650 + 0.8(Y - 0.2Y) - 0.3Y
Y = 1,650 + 0.8(0.8Y) - 0.3Y
Y = 1,650 + 0.64Y - 0.3Y
Y = 1,650 + 0.34Y
Y - 0.34Y = 1,650
0.66Y = 1,650
Y = 1,650/0.66
Y = 2,500
The equilibrium national income is 2,500.
(4)
Now, government increases its purchases to 700.
So, now G = 700.
At equilibrium
Y = C + I + G + X - IM
Y = 400 + 0.8YD + 100 + 700 + 650 - 0.3Y
Y = 400 + 0.8(Y - T) + 1,450 - 0.3Y
Y = 1,850 + 0.8(Y - 0.2Y) - 0.3Y
Y = 1,850 + 0.8(0.8Y) - 0.3Y
Y = 1,850 + 0.64Y - 0.3Y
Y = 1,850 + 0.34Y
Y - 0.34Y = 1,850
0.66Y = 1,850
Y = 1,850/0.66
Y = 2,803.03
The new equilibrium national income is 2,803.03

A) aggregate expenditure AE = C + I
AE = 2100 + 0.8Y + 500
AE = 2600 + 0.8Y
B) at equilibrium
AE = Y
2600 + 0.8Y = Y
Y = 2600/0.2 = 13000
Equilibrium income is 13000
C) national saving= investment when there is no government. Hans National saving is 500.
You can verify this
Private saving = disposable income - consumption
= 13000 - 2100 - 0.8*13000 = 500
There is no government which means public saving is zero and therefore private savings is equal to National savings and it is equal to 500.

Given that,

Bandwidth = 30Mbps = 30 * 106 bits per second

Segment-length = 1500B = 1500 * 8 bits

= 12000 bits

So to transfer each segment we need,

(Segment-length)/(Bandwidth) sec

= (12000)/(30 * 106)

= 0.4 ms

Given that propagation-delay = 420ms

a)

In Go-Back-N the sender sends N frames at a time. So sender window size = N

But reciever can recieve only one packet at a time. So receiver window size = 1

Minimum sequence numbers needed is equal to sum of sender and receiver window sizes

= N + 1

So minimum number of bits needed for sequence numbers = log2(N + 1)

b)

In Selective Repeat to achieve maximum efficiency sender should send (1 + 2*a) packets where,

a = (propagation-delay)/(transmission-delay)

= 420/0.4 = 1050

So 1 + 2*a = 1 + 2*1050 = 2101

So sender window size = 2101

In selective repeat, sender window size is equal to receiver window size.

So receiver window size = 2101

Minimum sequence numbers needed is sum of sender and receiver window size

= 2101 + 2101 = 4202

So minimum number of bits for sequence numbers needed = log2(4202) = 12.036

So we need 13 bits minimum for sequence numbers to achieve maximum efficiency in selective repeat protocol.

a) Annual percentage return (APR is the raw interest rate quoted. To get the effective interest rate that is received on investment, we need to take the effect of compounding. By taking the effect of such compounding, what we get is effective annual rate (EAR)
EAR = {(1 + APR/Compounding frequency)^Compounding frequency} - 1
b)
1) Bank X = 12% with monthly compounding
Compounding frequency = 12
EAR = {(1 + APR/Compounding frequency)^Compounding frequency} - 1
EAR = {(1 + 12%/12)^12} - 1
EAR = {(1 + 1%)^12} - 1
EAR = 1.12682
EAR = 0.12682
EAR = 12.68%
Bank Y = 13% with semi-annual compounding
Compounding frequency = 2
EAR = {(1 + APR/Compounding frequency)^Compounding frequency} - 1
EAR = {(1 + 13%/2)^2} - 1
EAR = {(1 + 6.5%)^2} - 1
EAR = 1.134225 - 1
EAR = 0.134225
EAR = 13.42%
On the basis of EAR, we will prefer bank Y over Bank X as it has a higher EAR which means the interest earned on deposit will be more than what will be earned in Bank X
ii)
Money invested in bank X for 5 years
Interest rate = 12%
Monthly interest rate = 12% / 12 = 1%
Total months in 5 years = 60 months
Amount deposit = $500,000
Amount at maturity = Deposit * (1 + Monthly interest rate)^Months
= 500,000 * (1 + 1%)^60
= 500,000 * 1.8166967
= 908,348.35
Thus amount after 5 years will be $908,348.35
iii) Under Bank X, interest is paid monthly, thus with monthly interest withdrawals the amount in the bank account will be not more than the amount deposit. Thus the amount will be $500,000 which is equal to amount deposit.

a) As per the Companies Act 2013, a tenure of an External Auditor is to be 5 Years for an individual Auditor and 10 Year for a auditor Firm, As the audit firm Wong, Chen & Co is the Auditor Firm of BA company for last 15 Year and there is a relation built in this 15 Years but its not complsory to remove the auditor until he have viloted the Sec 139(9).which is if the auditor fails to file the audited income statment Ie P& L and Bs in the income Tax dept then he will be forced by law to be removed, otherwise the audit committee of the BA company can take the call to change the auditor by issuing notice after complition of 5 Year tenure, if he is doing all his duties in a proper way with any biase then the will be no issue in maintaining the same auditor, actual he will be well aware of the company isssues and can able to give suggestion as and when requried.

b) If a BA companies Audit committee is will to change the external auditor, then by giving notice to the exsisting auditor can be removed after his tenure of 5 year and can be appointed new auditor for next 5 Years, but as per ICAI any person in relation like internal auditor, employee, and official advisor can not be appointed as external auditor, But a personal relation will not be an issue for appointing a new auditor, in this case BA company seeks advice from Mavis who is a friend and a partner of Tang & Mok CPAs & Co firm and BA company is appointing the firm Tang & Mok CPAs & Co for auditing which is not a official relative, Mavis is also a friend not appointed as a full time advicer therefor this appointment has no issue.

c) The procedure of getting appointed as an auditor in BA company are.
1. He should get a letter from BA company regarding his appointment.
2. He should send a acceptance in writting to BA Company.
3. He should send a communication to the pervious Auditor and get all documentation till that date.

2. Polemics : Everything portrayed in the Novel was the combined picture of a complex relationship, arts and real life situation. Somewhere it was enough with feel of perception but it lacks a lot of vision. The things pictured in the novel were much different from there actual feeling.
3. Everyday life: the novel connects with the real life to a much extent. Where the couple has nothing to do with the lighthouse but they express their relationship in such yes or no towards many things happening in a day life. It seems different to others from the inherent feeling in the sentences.
So As Virginia said that it has nothing to do with the lighthouse is 100% right because lighthouse is just a lighthouse present in everybody’s life in everyday in every situation.

Total Cost Function, TC = 5000 + 6q3 - 24q2 + 50q
a)
In economics, fixed costs, also known as overhead costs, are expenses that are not dependent on the level of goods produced by the business. They tend to remain fixed irrespective of the amount of goods produced.
In the above given total cost function, the first term, 5000 is fixed irrespective of the amount of coffee production. so it is fixed costs.
TFC = 5000
Examples of fixed costs are
- rent for shop for coffee production,
- investment on machinery for coffee production.
b)
Average cost funcion = Total Cost Function / q
= (5000 + 6q3 - 24q2 + 50q) / q
AC = 5000/q + 6q2 - 24q + 50
Marginal cost function, MC = d(Total Cost Function) / d(q) ....... d is derivaive
MC = d(5000 + 6q3 - 24q2 + 50q) / dq
MC = 18q2 - 48q + 50
c)
MC = 18q2 - 48q + 50
at q =10,
MC = 18(10)2 - 48(10) + 50
= 18(100) - 480 + 50
= 1800 - 480 + 50
= 1850 - 480
= 1370
at q =11,
MC = 18(11)2 - 48(11) + 50
= 18(121) - 528 + 50
= 2178 - 528 + 50
= 2228 - 528
= 1700
As we can see that with rise in quantity of coffee production from 10 o 11, the Marginal cost of producing 1 extra unit has increased. So with rise in quantity of coffee production MC will rise.
Law of Diminishing Marginal Returns Definition
The law of diminishing marginal returns states that adding an additional variable factor of production while keeping the other factor fixed, will result in a smaller increase in output for each variable factor in the short run. it implies that the Marginal product of extra labor will fall as we increase the units of labor. this fall in the marginal product of labor with fixed wages paid to extra labor causes cost of prodcuion of coffee production as well as MC to rise.
So, The law of diminishing marginal returns helps us explain rising Marginal costs.
In the coffee shop case, for example, there is only one machine for coffee production(i.e. Capital or fixed factor). As we add more and more labor( variable factor) to a given fixed factor, the output produced by each extra worker will be lower than the earlier worker.
Reasons for Diminishing marginal return to labor:
- a single machine with so many workers will lead to a lack of time for each worker to prepare coffee, so it reduces their productivity
- Employing extra workers. the shop may wish to serve more customers during the busy months. However, employing extra workers may create congestion due to lack of space in the shop and lead to a fall in MP of labor and rise in MC of coffee.
- increase in the number of workers also increases supervision costs.

Itemset: Set of items that occur together

Support: It gives the Frequency (number of times the item has occurred) of the item in the dataset.

Confidence: This explains how Y is purchased when X is purchased. It defines the association between two items.

For example: If a person buys milk then he is likely to buy eggs and bread as well or vice versa.

Lift: The lift is a measure of the performance of an association rule at predicting or classifying cases as having an enhanced response (with respect to the population as a whole), measured against a random choice association rule.

Association rule: Probability that particular items are purchased together.

Itemsets that meet minimum support are referred to as frequent itemsets.

SELECT S1.STAFFNO, S1.FNAME, S1.LNAME, S1.POSITION
FROM Staff S1
WHERE STAFFNO=
(SELECT DISTINCT S2.SV_STAFFNO
FROM Staff S2)
ORDER BY S1.LNAME ASC;
In this example, (SELECT DISTINCT S2.SV_STAFFNO FROM Staff S2) is the subquery to find the STAFFNO of all the staffs who have subordinates. The distinct keyword is used so that it can find distinct STAFFNOs. The WHERE clause helps to find the details of staffs who have subordinates. a) First order condition can be derived by differentiating the utility function w.r.t e and equating that to 0, doing so we get

(1-p)w - c'(e) = 0

=> c'(e) = (1-p) w

b) As p increases, it is more likely that charges would get a fixed payment of L irrespective of the effort he puts in. So Charles would prefer to put in less effort since he is more certain of receiving the fixed amount L. Similarly, as the probability to get the fixed payment reduces (p decreases), Charles is more incentivised to work harder that is put in more effort e to secure a payment w.e.

c) L is the amount of fixed payment. A higher L means a higher fixed income which would give lesser incentive to earn more by putting in more effort since he can meet his needs in a higher L. A lower L means that Charles would have to put in extra e to earn an amount that is sufficient for his living.

replies.msg:
Total Cost Function, TC = 5000 + 6q3 - 24q2 + 50q
a)
In economics, fixed costs, also known as overhead costs, are expenses that are not dependent on the level of goods produced by the business. They tend to remain fixed irrespective of the amount of goods produced.
In the above given total cost function, the first term, 5000 is fixed irrespective of the amount of coffee production. so it is fixed costs.
TFC = 5000
Examples of fixed costs are
- rent for shop for coffee production,
- investment on machinery for coffee production.
b)
Average cost funcion = Total Cost Function / q
= (5000 + 6q3 - 24q2 + 50q) / q
AC = 5000/q + 6q2 - 24q + 50
Marginal cost function, MC = d(Total Cost Function) / d(q) ....... d is derivaive
MC = d(5000 + 6q3 - 24q2 + 50q) / dq
MC = 18q2 - 48q + 50
c)
MC = 18q2 - 48q + 50
at q =10,
MC = 18(10)2 - 48(10) + 50
= 18(100) - 480 + 50
= 1800 - 480 + 50
= 1850 - 480
= 1370
at q =11,
MC = 18(11)2 - 48(11) + 50
= 18(121) - 528 + 50
= 2178 - 528 + 50
= 2228 - 528
= 1700
As we can see that with rise in quantity of coffee production from 10 o 11, the Marginal cost of producing 1 extra unit has increased. So with rise in quantity of coffee production MC will rise.
Law of Diminishing Marginal Returns Definition
The law of diminishing marginal returns states that adding an additional variable factor of production while keeping the other factor fixed, will result in a smaller increase in output for each variable factor in the short run. it implies that the Marginal product of extra labor will fall as we increase the units of labor. this fall in the marginal product of labor with fixed wages paid to extra labor causes cost of prodcuion of coffee production as well as MC to rise.
So, The law of diminishing marginal returns helps us explain rising Marginal costs.
In the coffee shop case, for example, there is only one machine for coffee production(i.e. Capital or fixed factor). As we add more and more labor( variable factor) to a given fixed factor, the output produced by each extra worker will be lower than the earlier worker.
Reasons for Diminishing marginal return to labor:
- a single machine with so many workers will lead to a lack of time for each worker to prepare coffee, so it reduces their productivity
- Employing extra workers. the shop may wish to serve more customers during the busy months. However, employing extra workers may create congestion due to lack of space in the shop and lead to a fall in MP of labor and rise in MC of coffee.
- increase in the number of workers also increases supervision costs.

Yes. As per Kantian ethics, Peter’s act of stealing drug from the store to cure his wife's terminal illness is justified, as the intention behind the act was to save a life. According to Kant, the intention behind the act is more important than the act itself.

According Kant's moral theory, the rightness or wrongness of actions does not depend on their consequences but on whether they fulfill our duty. Morality is based on free will and freedom of choice. In this case, Peter fulfilled his moral duty towards his wife in the best way possible. The chemist was selfish and was charging more money that is against morality. He was treating other people as a means to make money that was against Kant’s ethics. According to Kant’s theory, one must never treat people as a means to an end but as an end in themselves. Kant indicates that the motivation behind an action must be based on obligation and well thought. Here Peter's act was based on his obligation towards his wife.

The morality or righteousness v/s wrongness of an act is based on the act itself. The morality of a particular action is not related to its consequences. So actions are either intrinsically moral or not moral. The decisions about the act are based on whether or not the action is a moral one. People have to act as if the maxim of their actions is going to become a universal law of nature. Thee maxims must be consistent with the empirical facts of the natural world. In the above example, Peter’s actions may be viewed as wrong by some. But when we see the greedy chemist who charges double for the drug in spite of knowing that a person is dying, who is unwilling to help a dying person is against morality. It cannot be considered as a good act.

One main principle of Kant is don’t deceive others and do not harm them. We must do what is good for the self and what is good for others too. When we apply these rules, then we cat in accordance with the universal law. Whenever a person does something, he has to act in such a way that the cat will replace individual preferences with purely universal terms. Doing one’s duty is morality. When an action is done from one’s duty, then it is morally justified. So Peter’s action is morally justified.

replies.msg: Everyday we are faced with battling choices, these choices either exerts pain or pleasure. Choosing the choice that exerts pleasure over pain is the ultimate path to happiness. And this is the theory of utilitarianism, which was proposed to explain the act of morality in terms of what is right and what is wrong given the fact that a situation which increases the amount of pleasure and good results is the choice one should make while taking a decision. In the case of Mary, she and her husband were battling with the choice of either steal the drug or let Mary die of the rare disease. Because stealing is considered as a crime, but letting go off Mary due to financial troubles and the inability to buy the drug was too not acceptable and would result in painful outcomes, therefore according to the theory of utilitarianism the act of Peter would be considered justified as saving Mary's life would have higher utility than committing a crime. This theory opposes those actions that would result in unpleasurable outcomes and unhappiness. Though this choice would have an unpleasurable result for the chemist, but the amount of people who would be happy and will have pleasurable result for is more. Because act utilitarianism emphaisis on the effects of the choice made ie, whether the choice results in pain or pleasure and does not consider the types of actions a person has taken such as stealing, therefore in the above case as stealing of the drug would generate positive outcomes and will lead to happiness over unhappiness, according to the act utilitarianism this is ultimate choice that can be made.

def humanize secs
[
[60, :seconds],
[60, :minutes],
[24, :hours],
[365, :days],
[100, :years]
].map do |count, name|
if secs > 0

"#{n.to_i} #{name}"
end
end.compact.reverse.join(' ')
end
